fig_scree_factor_loadings.caption: Factor structure of LLM personality responses. Left: Scree plot showing eigenvalue spectrum with Kaiser criterion (dashed line). Only 3 factors exceed the threshold (eigenvalues: 6.80, 4.33, 2.99). Right: Factor loading heatmap for the 3-factor Varimax-rotated solution, showing Big Five domains collapsing into Extraversion/Sociability (F1), Neuroticism/Agreeableness (F2), and Conscientiousness/Impulsivity (F3).

fig_pir_sdr_mechanism.caption: Reverse-item inconsistency and acquiescence mechanism. Left: PIR by domain. Dashed line: overall mean PIR = 0.584. Right: Reverse-item agree rate versus PIR (r = 0.726), demonstrating acquiescence bias as the driving mechanism.

fig_variance_decomposition.caption: Sequential variance decomposition. Left: Likert scales (IPIP + SD3). Right: Binary scales (ZKPQ + EPQR). Inter-model variance accounts for only 0.3% of Likert and 0.5% of binary response variance.

fig_convergent_validity_forest.caption: Convergent validity: Spearman correlations between overlapping constructs. Extraversion-Sociability shows sign reversal (r = -0.09, expected r > 0.60). 7/8 pairs correct sign, but magnitudes weaker than human baselines.

fig_factor_collapse_summary.caption: Expected vs observed factor structure. Human: 5 separated Big Five factors. LLM: 3-factor collapse (Tucker phi = 0.990). Robust across all 18 models and all leave-one-out analyses.

fig_measurement_invariance.caption: Persona-level measurement invariance. Pearson r between Default and each MBTI persona profile. ENFJ closest (r = 0.608), ESTP farthest (r = 0.206).

fig_response_styles.caption: Response style profiles across 18 LLMs. Acquiescence gap ranges from 0.38 to 1.69 across models.

fig_cronbach_comparison.caption: Cronbach alpha comparison. Human: 0.87-0.90. LLM: -0.02 to 0.36. Synthetic baselines bracket the LLM range.

fig_pipeline_overview.caption: Experimental pipeline: 4 instruments (221 items) to 18 LLMs x 17 conditions to 67,626 responses to 6 psychometric analyses.
